_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of the functions and capabilities of internal teams that emulate threat activities to benefit the organization.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to maintain independence and objectivity when challenging models developed by senior or politically influential teams, providing effective challenge regardless of organizational dynamics.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-003 (LS, cosine: 0.485), AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.459).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.3/10 (raw 0.234). Shared vocabulary: [teams]. DCWF-only terms [activities, benefit, capabilities, emulate, functions, internal] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, challenge, challenging, developed, dynamics, effective] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.876 (P=0.886, R=0.866). P exceeds R by 0.020 — the DCWF element's 17 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:0.0%, N:79.9%, C:20.1%). No dominant classification for "Knowledge of the functions and capabilities of internal teams that emulate threa" vs "Ability to maintain independence and objectivity when challenging models develop" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.508 > STS 0.234 (gap +0.275). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [teams] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.8%. Shared tokens: [teams]. DCWF-only: [activities, benefit, capabilities, emulate, functions, internal]. WIDAI-only: [ability, challenge, challenging, developed, dynamics, effective].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.275 with low STS 0.234. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
